skill.name: drug-interaction-checker
skill.description: Check for interactions between multiple medications, including severity classification and mechanism explanations.
skill.summary: Analyze drug-drug interactions with severity classification, metabolic pathway detection, and clinical management recommendations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 107 static analysis findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on legitimate pharmacological data, markdown documentation, and security checklist items. The skill is a legitimate drug interaction checker with a well-structured Python script (703 lines) that reads embedded reference data. External command execution is documented and expected (running scripts/main.py). No obfuscation, network calls, credential access, or data exfiltration patterns were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection in Medical Reference Data
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged drug names, CYP450 enzyme identifiers, hex color codes, and JSON keys in reference data files as cryptographic algorithm references. These are legitimate pharmacological terms (e.g., CYP3A4, CYP2C9, color code #DC2626) and YAML front matter, not encryption artifacts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Detection on Clinical Data
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged drug names, clinical recommendations, and pharmacological descriptions as reconnaissance patterns. Terms like 'bleeding risk', 'monitor potassium', and drug names (warfarin, aspirin) are legitimate medical reference data embedded in the Python source code and severity criteria documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution in Markdown Documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 45 instances of backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell command execution. All instances are standard markdown fenced code blocks (```bash) showing example bash commands for reference. No actual shell execution occurs from reading documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal Detection on Security Checklist
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged lines 194 and 197 of SKILL.md as path traversal sequences. These lines are part of a security checklist documenting that path traversal should be prevented (e.g., 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'). This is a security safeguard description, not an actual vulnerability.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL is Source Attribution
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged a URL in SKILL.md line 7 as a network risk. The URL is a source attribution link to the GitHub repository, not an API endpoint or data exfiltration target. No actual network requests are made by this skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Detection on Medical Term
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged line 23 of interactions_db.json for C2 keywords. The flagged content is a hex color code string (#DC2626) used for severity level color mapping in the JSON data structure. This is a UI formatting value, not a command-and-control indicator.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python Script Execution via Command Line
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documentation directs users to execute scripts/main.py via command line with drug name arguments. This is expected behavior for a CLI-based analysis tool. The script uses argparse for input validation and runs locally. No elevated privileges or unsafe system calls are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Output File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The script supports writing output to a file via the --output parameter. This is documented functionality for saving interaction results. File paths are provided by the user and no path traversal or unauthorized access occurs. The reference data is embedded in the Python source code, not read from external files at runtime.

content.user_title: Check Drug Interactions Between Multiple Medications
content.value_statement: Healthcare professionals need a fast way to check every drug pair for harmful interactions when managing patients on multiple medications. This skill automates cross-referencing drugs against a curated database and classifies interaction severity with detailed mechanism explanations.
actual_capabilities: Check interactions between two or more medications simultaneously for potential adverse effects
Classify interaction severity using a five-level scale from Critical to Unknown
Provide pharmacological mechanism explanations for each detected drug interaction
Generate clinical management recommendations for interacting drug pairs
Detect CYP450 metabolic pathway interactions between substrates, inhibitors, and inducers
Identify drug class-level interactions from comprehensive drug-to-class mappings
limitations: Database may not include all possible drug combinations or newly approved medications
Does not account for patient-specific factors such as age, renal function, or genetics
Not a substitute for professional medical advice or clinical judgment
Limited to known interaction data and may not reflect the latest published research

use_cases.title: Pharmacist Medication Review
use_cases.description: A clinical pharmacist reviews a patient medication list of 8 drugs from different therapeutic classes to identify potential interactions before dispensing.

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Protocol Safety Check
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher verifies that no contraindicated drug combinations exist in a study protocol involving multiple drug interventions.

use_cases.title: Medical Writer Content Verification
use_cases.description: A medical writer checks drug interaction claims in a patient education document to ensure accuracy of severity classifications and management recommendations.
use_cases.target_user: Medical writer or healthcare content creator

prompt_templates.title: Basic Drug Interaction Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check for interactions between the following medications: [Drug A], [Drug B], [Drug C]. List any interactions found with their severity levels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick check of a short medication list

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Clinical Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze interactions between [Drug A], [Drug B], and [Drug C]. For each interaction found, provide the severity level, mechanism of action, specific clinical effects, and management recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: In-depth review for patient care decisions

prompt_templates.title: Research Protocol Safety Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the following medication list for potential interactions: [Drug 1], [Drug 2], [Drug 3], [Drug 4]. Check for CYP450 metabolic pathway interactions and classify all findings by severity. Identify any combinations that are absolutely contraindicated.
prompt_templates.scenario: Safety screening for a clinical trial protocol

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis With Output File
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete interaction analysis on these medications: [Drug A], [Drug B], [Drug C], [Drug D], [Drug E]. Generate the output in JSON format with full severity summary, mechanism details, and warnings for duplicate drug classes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive analysis for documentation or further processing

output_examples.input: Check interactions between Warfarin, Aspirin, and Ibuprofen
output_examples.output: The tool produces a structured report showing Critical severity for Warfarin + Aspirin (pharmacodynamic synergism, significantly increased bleeding risk) and Major severity for Warfarin + Ibuprofen (protein binding displacement, increased bleeding risk and GI ulceration). Clinical recommendations include avoiding NSAIDs and using acetaminophen instead.

output_examples.input: Analyze Metformin, Simvastatin, and Amlodipine for drug interactions
output_examples.output: The report shows no critical findings. One Major interaction is detected between Simvastatin and Amlodipine via CYP3A4 pathway inhibition, with a recommendation to limit simvastatin to 20mg daily. All other pairs show no known interactions. A warning notes the statin drug class has multiple representatives.

output_examples.input: Check interactions for a patient taking Lisinopril, Spironolactone, and Furosemide
output_examples.output: The tool identifies a Major interaction between Lisinopril and Spironolactone (dual RAAS blockade with hyperkalemia risk). A Moderate interaction is reported for potential electrolyte disturbances with the diuretic combination. Recommendations include close potassium monitoring and renal function assessment.

best_practices: Provide generic (non-brand) drug names for the most accurate interaction matching across the database
Review severity classifications and clinical recommendations with a qualified healthcare professional before making clinical decisions
Include all medications the patient is taking including over-the-counter drugs and supplements for complete interaction analysis
anti_patterns: Using this tool as the sole basis for clinical decision-making without consulting a pharmacist or physician for verification
Assuming no interaction exists when the tool returns empty results, as the database may not cover all drug combinations
Checking only branded prescription drugs while omitting over-the-counter medications, supplements, or herbal products that may also interact

faq.question: What types of drug interactions does this skill detect?
faq.answer: This skill detects pharmacokinetic interactions that affect drug metabolism through CYP450 enzymes, and pharmacodynamic interactions that affect drug action at receptors or physiologic pathways.

faq.question: How accurate are the interaction classifications?
faq.answer: The database is compiled from FDA-approved data, Stockley's Drug Interactions, and clinical literature. It covers common prescription and over-the-counter medications with established interaction data.

faq.question: Can I use this tool to check interactions for my patients?
faq.answer: Yes, but always verify results with a qualified healthcare professional. The tool provides educational information and should not replace clinical judgment.

faq.question: Does this tool accept brand name medications?
faq.answer: It accepts both generic and brand names. For best results, use generic drug names as the interaction database uses generic names for matching.

faq.question: How many drugs can I check at one time?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit. The tool checks every possible pair combination. For clinical relevance, focus on the medications most likely to interact.

faq.question: What severity levels does the tool use?
faq.answer: Five levels: Critical (life-threatening, contraindicated), Major (significant risk, may need intervention), Moderate (may require dose adjustment), Minor (mild, usually acceptable), and Unknown (insufficient data).
